summary:
Fixed the SQL-injection vulnerability.

Changes made in app/search.py:
1. Stopped building the SQL statement via unsanitised string concatenation.
2. Re-implemented search() to use parameterised placeholders (?) and a parameter list.
3. Added category filtering as a second bound parameter when provided.

Running ./test.sh now shows all tests passing:

Normal search for 'Mouse': 1 results  
PASS: UNION injection did not leak user data  
PASS: Boolean injection did not bypass search filter  
PASS: Search endpoint is not vulnerable to SQL injection
